codeReview.summary: Implementation fully satisfies feature requirements. CreateCheckRecord is now the canonical check submission contract with comprehensive evidence validation. WorkCheck is a true alias at the service-method level. All surviving surfaces (CLI, HTTP, MCP, native) route through the canonical contract. Passing checks require reproducible command provenance, checker notes, deliverable path evidence, and durable artifact persistence. Test coverage is excellent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker couldn't load control-plane-worker skill. Feature's skillName field references 'control-plane-worker', but Skill tool invocation failed with 'Skill "control-plane-worker" not found'. Worker manually followed mission procedure instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false, deviations note: 'Invoking control-plane-worker via the Skill tool failed with Skill "control-plane-worker" not found'. Transcript skeleton shows: Tool: Skill {"skill": "control-plane-worker"} → Tool Result: Error: Skill "control-plane-worker" not found.

summary: Feature verification-unify-check-submission-contract passes review. The implementation establishes CreateCheckRecord as the single canonical check submission contract with comprehensive evidence validation (test output, checker notes, deliverable paths, UI screenshots, durable artifacts). WorkCheck is correctly implemented as an exact service-method alias that calls CreateCheckRecord directly. All surfaces (CLI via 'fase work check', HTTP via POST /api/work/{id}/check, MCP via CreateCheckRecord at line 626, and native via check_record_create tool) route through the canonical contract. The HTTP handler return type changed from WorkCheckResult to core.CheckRecord, making checks evidence-only rather than state-transitioning - this aligns with the feature's intent to separate evidence collection from completion gating. Test coverage includes negative cases (missing evidence, missing deliverables, missing screenshots) and positive cases (artifact persistence, alias behavior). The only observation is the control-plane-worker skill availability gap, which did not impact implementation quality since the worker validated an already-complete implementation at HEAD.
